alerts.headline: Heat Advisory issued July 21 at 3:19PM PDT until July 22 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS Seattle WA
alerts.description:
* WHAT...Hot conditions with high temperatures in the mid to upper
90s expected and low temperatures from 60 to 65 degrees. This will
pose a major risk of heat-related illness.

* WHERE...Eastern Kitsap County, City of Seattle, Downtown Everett /
Marysville Area, Eastside, Foothills and Valleys of Central King
County, Foothills and Valleys of Pierce and Southern King
Counties, Foothills and Valleys of Snohomish and Northern King
Counties, Lowlands of Pierce and Southern King Counties, and
Shorel
alerts.instruction:
Drink plenty of fluids, stay in an air-conditioned room, stay out of
the sun, and check up on relatives and neighbors. Young children and
pets should never be left unattended in vehicles under any
circumstances.

Take extra precautions if you work or spend time outside. When
possible reschedule stre

alerts.headline: Flood Watch issued July 21 at 3:40PM PDT until July 21 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS Reno NV
alerts.description:
* WHAT...Flash flooding caused by excessive rainfall is possible due
to thunderstorms.

* WHERE...Portions of east central California, including the
following areas, Greater Lake Tahoe Area and Mono County and
western Nevada, including the following areas, Greater Lake Tahoe
Area, Greater Reno-Carson City-Minden Area and Mineral and
Southern Lyon Counties.

* WHEN...Until 11 PM PDT this evening.

* IMPACTS...Excessive runoff may result in flooding of creeks,
streams, urban areas, burn scars, and
alerts.instruction:
You should monitor later forecasts and be prepared to take action
should Flash Flood Warnings be issued.

alerts.headline: Special Weather Statement issued July 21 at 3:22PM PDT by NWS Reno NV
alerts.description:
At 319 PM PDT, strong thunderstorms are ongoing across western
Nevada and portions of the eastern Sierra, increasing coverage
northward throughout the afternoon.

HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 40 mph and half inch hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around
unsecured objects. Minor hail damage to vegetation is
possible.

Locations impacted include...
Reno, Carson City, South Lake Tahoe, Fallon, Lovelock, Tahoe City,
Kings Beach, Gardnerville, Vi
alerts.instruction:
If outdoors, consider seeking shelter inside a building.

Torrential rainfall is also occurring with this storm and may lead to
localized flooding. Do not drive your vehicle through flooded
roadways.

alerts.headline: Extreme Heat Warning issued July 21 at 1:12PM PDT until July 27 at 8:00PM PDT by NWS Las Vegas NV
alerts.description:
* WHAT...Dangerously hot conditions expected. Afternoon temperatures
in the desert valleys will climb into the 108 to 114 degree range,
and may exceed 115 along the Colorado River Valley and 120 in Death
Valley. Overnight lows generally 80 to 95 degrees.

* WHERE...Portions of northwest Arizona, southeast California, and
southern Nevada including the Greater Mojave Desert region.

* WHEN...From 10 AM PDT / MST Thursday to 8 PM PDT / MST Monday.

* IMPACTS...Heat related illnesses increase signif
alerts.instruction:
Drink plenty of fluids, stay in an air-conditioned room, stay out of
the sun, and check up on relatives and neighbors.

Do not leave young children and pets in unattended vehicles. Car
interiors will reach lethal temperatures in a matter of minutes.
